label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is whether following a series of instructions will bring me back to the starting point. The instructions are: 1. Turn around. 2. Turn left. 3. Take 1 step. 4. Take 2 steps. 5. Turn around. 6. Turn around. 7. Turn around. 8. Take 3 steps. I need to track my direction and position as I go through each step. Let's assume I start facing North. So my initial direction is North, and my position is (0, 0). 1. Turn around: From North, turning around would face me South. 2. Turn left: From South, turning left would face me East. 3. Take 1 step: Moving 1 step East, so new position is (1, 0). 4. Take 2 steps: Still facing East, so moving 2 more steps to (3, 0). 5. Turn around: From East, turning around would face me West. 6. Turn around: From West, turning around would face me East. 7. Turn around: From East, turning around would face me West. 8. Take 3 steps: Moving 3 steps West, so new position is (3 - 3, 0) = (0, 0). So yes, I end up back at the starting point (0, 0). The answer should be "Yes." Actually, let me double check my logic about turning around. When facing a direction and turning around, it's a 180-degree turn. So: - North -> South - South -> North - East -> West - West -> East For the turns: 1. Start facing North 2. Turn around -> facing South 3. Turn left -> facing East (left from South is East) 4. Take 1 step East -> (1, 0) 5. Take 2 steps East -> (3, 0) 6. Turn around -> facing West 7. Turn around
